c74object.name: tap.spektral~
c74object.digest: Spectral delay: per-band level, delay, and feedback across the spectrum
c74object.description: The tap.spektral~ object is a recreation of Native Instruments' Spektral Delay (2001, discontinued). The signal is split by a short-time Fourier transform into log-spaced frequency bands (default 160, like the original), and every band has its own level, delay time (up to 12 seconds), and feedback amount — set by sending curves as lists of any length (automatically resampled to the band count, so a multislider or function output drives them directly) or by poking single bands. The freeze attribute mutes the matrix input and recirculates the delay memory losslessly, looping its contents indefinitely — the original's signature button. Two frequency-domain Mod FX process the spectrum before the delay matrix: rotate cyclically shifts the bins over time, and smear diffuses the bin amplitudes (the original's "reverb simulation by smearing the amplitude courses"). An LFO can animate a band shift, the feedback master, or the delay scale. All parameters and curves interpolate smoothly (curves at the frame rate, crossfaded by the overlap-add), and sixteen preset slots — including the full curves — can be stored and morphed between over a settable time. The STFT uses a Hann window at 4x overlap with exact reconstruction; latency is one FFT frame, and the dry path is delayed to match so the mix stays time-aligned. The first argument sets the FFT size (a power of two, 256-8192, default 2048). Single-channel: wrap in mc. for stereo (the original's independent left/right curves are two instances).

objarg.name: fftsize
objarg.description: FFT size: a power of two, 256-8192 (default 2048). The hop is a quarter of this; per-band delays quantize to the hop, and the latency is one FFT frame.

method.name: gains
method.description: Set the per-band level curve (0-2). A list of any length is linearly resampled to the band count; alternatively an integer band index followed by a value sets one band (0-based).

method.name: delays
method.description: Set the per-band delay curve in milliseconds (0-12000), same list/poke forms as gains. Delays quantize to the STFT hop (fftsize/4 samples).

method.name: fbs
method.description: Set the per-band feedback curve (0-0.99), same list/poke forms as gains.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters and all three curves in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters and curves to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds (default: the interp attribute).

method.name: clear
method.description: Clear the delay matrix and all STFT state.

attribute.name: bands
attribute.description: Number of editable log-spaced bands (8-512, default 160). Curves keep their indices when this changes; the band frequencies shift.

attribute.name: level
attribute.description: Master multiplier (0-2) on the per-band level curve.

attribute.name: fb
attribute.description: Master multiplier (0-2) on the per-band feedback curve (per-bin feedback is capped at 0.99).

attribute.name: delayscale
attribute.description: Master multiplier (0-2) on the per-band delay curve.

attribute.name: freeze
attribute.description: Freeze: mute the matrix input and recirculate the delay memory losslessly — the contents loop indefinitely until released.

attribute.name: rotate
attribute.description: Cyclic spectral rotation in bins per second (-500..500). 0 is off.

attribute.name: smear
attribute.description: Amplitude smear (0-100): per-bin peak-hold diffusion over time, from off (0) through reverb-like (mid) to near-freezing (100).

attribute.name: lfodepth
attribute.description: LFO depth (0-100): bins of shift for the bandshift destination; percent for the feedback and delayscale destinations.

attribute.name: lfodest
attribute.description: LFO destination: 0 = off, 1 = bandshift, 2 = feedback master, 3 = delay scale. (The original offered three simultaneous slots; one at a time here.)

attribute.name: mix
attribute.description: Dry/wet crossfade (0 = dry, 100 = wet), equal-power. The dry path is delayed by one FFT frame so it stays aligned with the wet path.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Interpolation time in milliseconds applied to direct parameter and curve changes.
